arts.sum: The Belgian Pro League has announced the next six nominees for the hall of fame. Johan Boskamp, Lei Clijsters, Heleen Jaques, Raoul Lambert, Robbie Rensenbrink and Wilfried Van Moer have…

arts.ttl: Sheffield Wednesday injury check on trio ahead of Stoke City clash
arts.sum: Sheffield Wednesday will make late calls on three players including captain Barry Bannan ahead of Friday's Championship match at Stoke City. Stoke follow this match with a trip to Elland Road to take on Leeds United on Monday before returning home to host Sheffield United next Friday and finishing the campaign away at Derby County.

arts.ttl: Barry Bannan update emerges as Sheffield Wednesday sweat over two new injury doubts for Stoke City tie
arts.sum: Sheffield Wednesday trio Barry Bannan, Michael Ihiekwe and Anthony Musaba are doubts for the Good Friday clash with… The post Barry Bannan update emerges as Sheffield Wednesday sweat over two new injury doubts for Stoke City tie appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Championship: Predictions for all 12 games on Friday
arts.sum: The Easter weekend double header in the Championship usually has major repercussions at both ends of the table and Tom McGarry is on hand to provide a score prediction for all 12 games in the second tier on Good Friday.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins' response to long throw question as he prepares for Stoke City 'honour'
arts.sum: Mark Robins is going to exploit or utilise everything he can for the good of Stoke City, whether it’s a long throw or a full house. “We go to Leeds on Monday and it’s Sheffield United on Friday night and both are trying to get automatic promotion although in Sheffield United’s last three games they have slipped up a little bit but they’ve got good players.

arts.ttl: Predicted Stoke City starting XI vs Sheffield Wednesday as Mark Robins finds consistency
arts.sum: “I think it’s important (to have that consistency) in any case but it’s been important for us because those players have got good experience, good ability, really good qualities and they have been able to put some really solid, good performances in and, at times, some really good performances,” he said ahead of a Good Friday clash with Sheffield Wednesday (3pm). Robins might know the importance of being able to have a settled side but that was, he admits, impossible for the first few weeks after he arrived in January and there were so many players either in the treatment room or building up fitness after a treatment room stay.

arts.ttl: Stoke City sell-out is 'music to Mark Robins' ears' as he talks building blocks, fun and full voice
arts.sum: Mark Robins says the chance to go out and play in front of a near-sold out bet365 Stadium is music to his ears as he looks for a win that will pull Stoke City a long way towards Championship safety. “The club has done a great job in getting them in, pricing wise and things like that, but also excitement growing around how we’re playing, results are picking up and belief is starting to grow.
